steps.title: Write the volume formula for a pyramid with a square base
steps.content: For a pyramid, $V = \frac{1}{3} \cdot \text{(base area)} \cdot \text{height}$. Since the base is a square with side length $s$, the base area is $s^2$. Substituting $V = 196$ cubic inches and height $= 12$ inches: $$196 = \frac{1}{3} \cdot s^2 \cdot 12$$

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content: Simplify the fraction on the right: $$196 = \frac{12s^2}{3} = 4s^2$$ Divide both sides by 4: $$s^2 = 49$$ Take the positive square root: $$s = 7 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Calculate the perimeter
steps.content: The perimeter of a square with side length $s$ is $P = 4s$. Substitute $s = 7$: $$P = 4 \cdot 7 = 28 \text{ inches}$$
